mechanism_narrative: Persistently low FEDFUNDS keeps the policy path anchored, reducing near-term discount-rate pressure and supporting risk appetite; over the next 20 trading days this aligns with falling 10-year Treasury yields if inflation does not reaccelerate.
failure_condition: Reject if 10-year yields rise over 20 trading days in a majority of low-FEDFUNDS observations, or if the sign flips positive when CPIAUCSL is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises above its recent trend while FEDFUNDS remains near the effective lower bound, the market prices earlier policy normalization and higher term premia, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher within 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above 2.5% while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%
regime_hint: reflation scare with near-zero policy rates
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances do not produce positive 20d yield changes more often than negative ones, or if higher CPIAUCSL fails to coincide with higher yields conditional on unchanged FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with weakening PAYEMS signals slackening demand and a softer growth outlook, which lowers expected real rates and drags 10-year Treasury yields down over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp over 2 months and PAYEMS falls on the latest monthly print
failure_condition: Reject if combined labor-softening triggers are followed by rising 10-year yields in more than 60% of cases, or if PAYEMS weakness without higher UNRATE does not materially weaken the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in RSAFS indicates softer consumer demand and weaker nominal spending, which lowers expected growth and inflation, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Reject if negative RSAFS trends are followed by higher 10-year yields more than half the time, or if the relation disappears after controlling for CPIAUCSL and UNRATE direction.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO growth with stable HOUST implies stronger real activity and tighter resource utilization, which lifts expected growth and inflation compensation and pushes 10-year Treasury yields higher over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month average and HOUST stops declining month over month
failure_condition: Reject if concurrent INDPRO and HOUST improvement does not precede positive 20d yield changes, or if the yield response is absent when both series strengthen together.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS is still growing but UNRATE stops falling, labor-market momentum is losing steam before it appears in other aggregates; this early slowdown lowers rate expectations and drives 10-year Treasury yields lower over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises or holds flat after a multi-month decline while PAYEMS growth slows
failure_condition: Reject if this early inflection pattern fails to precede lower yields on a 5-day horizon, or if PAYEMS and UNRATE together do not add explanatory power beyond either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, an upward move in FEDFUNDS transmits directly into higher expected short rates and a steeper term structure, raising 10-year Treasury yields over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS has increased in at least two consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive FEDFUNDS increases are not followed by higher 60d yields in a majority of cases, or if the sign is neutral when inflation is unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in GDPC1 and RSAFS marks broad-based demand acceleration, lifting inflation expectations and term premia; over a 60-day horizon this should push 10-year Treasury yields higher.
candidate_trigger: GDP growth and retail sales both accelerate versus their prior observation
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDP and retail-sales acceleration does not precede higher 60d yields, or if the yield response is positive only when one series improves but not both together.
